nonWriterNotice: This packet plans eval fixtures but does not write host-owned fixtures, prompts, runners, or policy.

planSummary.zeroPlanExpectation: Zero eval plans can be expected when reviewed eval-ready claims are already satisfied or when remaining reviewed claims are not Cautilus eval targets.

sourceRefs.excerpt: Use when you change a skill or agent and want to know whether it still triggers on the right prompts, executes cleanly, and keeps its static validation passing.

The same preset can evaluate a multi-turn agent episode when the fixture provides `turns`; Cautilus's own first-scan, refresh-flow, review-prepare, reviewer-launch, and review-to-eval dogfood fixtures derive their results from audit packets.

unresolvedQuestions: If the review-to-eval dogfood fixture is now part of the public promise, the README sentence may need to include it alongside first-scan, refresh-flow, review-prepare, and reviewer-launch.

sourceRefs.excerpt: Agent track — Claude / Codex plugin.** The `cautilus install` step also lands a bundled skill at `.agents/skills/cautilus/` with Claude and Codex plugin manifests, so an in-editor agent can drive the same contracts conversationally.

unresolvedQuestions: Should discovery split this into a deterministic install/materialization claim and a separate dev/skill conversational-driving claim?

sourceRefs.excerpt: It emits `cautilus.claim_eval_plan.v1` from reviewed `cautilus-eval` claims that are ready to verify, while preserving the host boundary by not writing fixtures, prompts, runners, wrappers, or policy.

unresolvedQuestions: Claude parity for this review-to-eval branch remains outside this evidence bundle.
